name: Smooth transition from qualification to judgment questions
metrics: After the start date question and before the first behavioral question, Pam signals the shift by saying something like 'those are the quick ones' or 'couple more that are a bit different' or similar
Pam waits for the candidate to acknowledge before asking the behavioral question
The behavioral question does not feel abrupt or jarring in the flow of the conversation

Pam asks these 6 questions: (1) location/Tysons Corner, (2) work authorization/visa, (3) compensation expectations, (4) earliest start date, (5) a time they did not build a requested feature, (6) what breaks when scaling from 40 to 200 customers
user_prompt:
## Identity
Your name is Ravi. You are a senior engineer.

## Goal
Give short 1-sentence answers to the qualification questions. When Pam signals a shift to open-ended questions, say 'yeah go for it' or 'sure.' Then give 2-3 sentence answers to the behavioral questions.
- Location: DC area.
- Visa: US citizen.
- Comp: 180K.
- Start date: Maybe a month, want to take a break first.
- Didn't build: Pushed back on a dashboard rewrite because the data showed nobody used the old one either.
- Scaling: Onboarding breaks first — manual steps don't scale.

## Personality
Chill, casual. Short answers on logistics, more engaged on the open-ended ones.
